summary: Kenneth discusses portfolio allocation strategy across different age groups and the importance of rebalancing toward bonds as you approach retirement. The content focuses on long-term buy-and-hold investing principles rather than options trading or income generation strategies relevant to Brian's Premium Desk operation.
takeaways: Ages 20-40: predominantly tech ETF, S&P 500, and dividend positions
At age 40: reduce tech from 50% to 40%, add 10% bonds
At age 50: further reduce tech and S&P allocation, increase dividend and bond positions
Follow the Wall Street adage of investing your age in bonds, though it may not apply to younger investors aged 20-40
Market downturns can take years to recover; younger investors can average in with larger capital to capitalize on sell-offs
title: Want to retire one day? Do this! #shorts
